pred: The proof that multi-head self-attention (MHSA) is at least as powerful as a convolution layer is based on the ability of MHSA to express the output of any convolutional layer with a certain number of output channels. Specifically, it is stated that any \(D_h\) out of \(D_{\textit {out}}\) outputs of the MHSA can express the output of any convolutional layer with \(D_h\) output channels. The main theorem asserts that the output channels of the convolutional layer should be \(\min (D_h, D_{\textit {out}})\). This indicates that MHSA can match or exceed the expressiveness of convolutional layers by appropriately configuring the dimensions of the heads and the output channels.
